subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.1.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of the Nature of God as the Creator
indicator_description: Describe the attributes of God
examplars:
1. Learners in groups discuss the attributes of God e.g. love, patience, merciful, Omniscient- All-knowing.
2. Learners role play scenarios to reflect the attributes of God in their lives.
3. Learners write essays on the attributes of God in relations to themselves.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of challenges that occur during adolescence
indicator_description: Explain problems associated with adolescence
examplars:
1. Learners talk about and list changes that occur during adolescence.
2. Learners think-pair-share on challenges associated with adolescence.
3. Learners illustrate challenges facing adolescence using the Future's Wheel.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of challenges that occur during adolescence
indicator_description: Explain how to manage the problems that occur during adolescence.
examplars:
1. Learners in groups discuss how to manage the challenges that come with changes during adolescence.
2. Groups to present reports for class discussion.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.1.2.2. Demonstrate understanding of personal hygiene during adolescence
indicator_description: Explain how to maintain personal hygiene during adolescence
examplars:
1. Learners think-pair-share on how to maintain personal hygiene during adolescence.
2. Learners discuss how adolescent problems affect national development.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.1.2.3. Demonstrate how adolescents can harness their potentials to achieve their goals in life
indicator_description: Explain how to prepare and accomplish aspirations in life
examplars:
1. Learners identify what they want to become in life and discuss why they chose particular vocations.
2. Learners share their views about how they intend to prepare themselves towards achieving their aspirations.
3. Learners in groups discuss things that could prevent them from achieving their goal.
4. Learners role play scenarios on how to overcome challenges.
5. Learners write essay on skills necessary for achieving aspirations.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of attitudes and behaviours of a responsible family member
indicator_description: Explain the need for cordial relationships among family members
examplars:
1. Learners in groups, discuss attitudes and behaviours of a responsible family member.
2. Learners discuss the importance of being committed to the family.
3. Learners engage in role-play and other activities that will teach them to become family members who are committed.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.1.4.1. Demonstrate knowledge of food safety practices
indicator_description: Identify ways of promoting food safely
examplars:
1. Learners discuss ways of ensuring food safely e.g. wash hands well, cover food well, serve in clean plates, serve cooked food hot.
2. Learners demonstrate how to cook and serve hot food safely.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.2.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of the importance of trees in our environment
indicator_description: Plant trees in their communities as a civic duty
examplars:
1. Learners discuss the importance of trees in their communities.
2. Learners undertake a tree planting project in the school to check erosion or provide shade.
3. Write down the processes about their project.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.2.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of the importance of animals to plants in the environment
indicator_description: Explain the importance of animal waste to plants
examplars:
1. Learners watch pictures/videos of manure and discuss the importance of manure.
2. Obtain different plant and animal waste from the community and use such materials to prepare manure.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.2.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of the map of Ghana
indicator_description: Identify the political regions on a sketch map of Ghana
examplars:
1. Learners talk about the political regions of Ghana e.g. the sixteen regions, their names and capitals.
2. Learners show the locations of the political regions on a sketch map of Ghana.
3. Observe a map of Africa and write out the countries, which border Ghana to the North, East and West.
4. Compose a song that locates the position of Ghana in relation to other countries.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.2.4.1. Show understanding of internal migration in Ghana
indicator_description: Describe internal migration in Ghana
examplars:
1. Learners explain migration as the movement of people from one place to another.
2. Learners in groups discuss the patterns of internal migration in Ghana.
3. Learners brainstorm the reasons for migration Ghana.
4. Learners show the migration pattern on a sketch map of Ghana.
5. Learners brainstorm and come out with challenges associated with urban settlements.
6. In groups discuss measures that can be taken to solve challenges associated with rural and urban settlements.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.3.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of the importance of prayer, worship and other acts of worship
indicator_description: Describe the importance of prayer in our lives
examplars:
1. Learners discuss the meaning of prayer.
2. Learners talk about the types of prayer.
3. Learners discuss the importance of prayer.
4. Learners demonstrate how prayer is done in the three major religions.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.3.2.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the significance of celebrations in Ghana
indicator_description: Identify two ways of making festivals beneficial to the communities
examplars:
1. Learners talk about ways of making celebrations of festivals beneficial to the communities.
2. Learners draw a durbar of chiefs during festival celebrations.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.3.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of fundamental human rights
indicator_description: Identify sources of help
examplars:
1. Learners discuss human rights abuses against children.
2. Learners discuss how to get help in case of abuse.
3. Learners discuss how to speak out and not keep quiet over an abuse.
4. Learners create posters speaking against child abuse.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.3.4.1. Demonstrate understanding of the latter lives of the leaders of the three major religions in Ghana
indicator_description: Narrate the story of the latter lives of the leaders of the major religions
examplars:
1. Learners discuss the latter lives of the religious leaders: Jesus Christ, The Holy Prophet Muhammad, Traditional leader.
2. Show pictures or video clips on the latter lives of Jesus Christ and a traditional leader.
3. Learners in groups explore moral lessons in the latter lives of the religious leaders.
4. Learners role play the moral lessons they have learnt.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.4.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of behaviours and attitudes for peaceful living
indicator_description: Describe activities for peaceful living
examplars:
1. Learners discuss ways of living peacefully with each other.
2. Learners discuss the importance of peaceful living.
3. Learners engage in a role-play and other activities to demonstrate peaceful living.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.4.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of behaviours and attitudes for peaceful living
indicator_description: Describe skills for resolving conflict in the family
examplars:
1. Learners explain domestic violence and conflict.
2. Learners discuss the causes of domestic violence and conflict in the family.
3. Learners share experiences of domestic violence and conflict in the community.
4. Discuss the effects of domestic violence on the family.
5. Learners role play peaceful resolution of conflict.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.4.1.2. Demonstrate understanding of the importance of social accountability
indicator_description: Explain the importance of public accountability
examplars:
1. Learners explain public accountability.
2. Learners discuss the importance of public accountability in promoting social justice.
3. In groups learners identify ways by which people can be held accountable for their stewardship.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.4.2.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the need to obey and submit to authority
indicator_description: Identify the role of children in promoting harmony with other family members
examplars:
1. Learners discuss what it takes to live in harmony.
2. Learners in pairs, list roles they can play to promote harmony in the family.
3. Let learners in pairs, list roles other family members can play to ensure harmony.
4. Ask learners to role-play the duties of their relatives in moulding their character.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.4.2.2. Demonstrate understanding of the need for good governance
indicator_description: Explain and appreciate the importance of democratic governance
examplars:
1. Learners identify various persons and bodies who are responsible in governing their school.
2. Learners discuss the importance of good governance in their school.
3. Learners in groups suggest ways in which school governance could be improved.
4. Class mock election: Learners demonstrate how to elect a leader in a democratic way.
5. Learners discuss why good governance is important for national development.
6. Learners discuss the features of democratic governance in Ghana.
7. Learners discuss how individuals can promote democratic governance.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.4.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of individual responsibilities in protecting water bodies
indicator_description: Identify individual responsibilities in protecting water bodies
examplars:
1. Learners watch pictures/videos on strategies to protect water bodies.
2. Learners discuss the pictures/videos and suggest individual responsibilities.
3. Learners form protecting water bodies clubs to demonstrate their responsibilities.
4. Learners undertake tree planting exercise along water bodies.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.4.6.1. Demonstrate knowledge of job opportunities in the agricultural value chain
indicator_description: Describe the agricultural value chain and the job opportunities
examplars:
1. Learners discuss the meaning of agricultural value chain.
2. Learners talk about job opportunities in the agricultural value chain e.g. crop farming, livestock, fisheries, Agro-chemicals industry, meat processing.
3. Learners write essays on their choice of job opportunities in the agricultural value chain.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.5.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of how Ghana cooperates with other nations
indicator_description: Explain how Ghana co-operates with other nations
examplars:
1. Learners recall countries that surround Ghana.
2. Learners identify countries surrounding Ghana on a map of West Africa.
3. Learners discuss how Ghana co-operates with her neighbours e.g. trade, sports, political.
4. Learners talk about the reasons why Ghana should continue to co-operate with other nations.
5. Learners dramatise the processes to follow in accepting help from other nations.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B6.5.1.2. Demonstrate understanding of climate change
indicator_description: Investigate the effects climate change on the environment
examplars:
1. Use video clips, field trips and pictures to help learners identify the effects of over concentration of greenhouse gases and climate change on the environment.
2. Discuss human activities in their community that contribute to the emission of greenhouse gases.
3. Learners in groups suggest ways by which human beings can adapt to climate change.
